_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to discuss the plot, characters and settings of stories and identify how language is used to bring these features to life.

_source.w: We are learning to discuss the plot, characters and settings of stories and identify how language is used to bring these features to life.
y: Noticing how authors use language helps us learn from their craft.
Understanding how language creates characters and settings deepens our enjoyment of stories.
This helps us use similar language techniques in our own writing.
eg: We find the words an author uses to describe a character's personality and discuss their effect.
We identify how the author uses language to make the setting feel real — like 'the cold wind howled'.
We trace the plot of a story, noting the key events and how language builds tension.
We discuss how an author's word choices make us feel about a character.
We highlight descriptive language in a text and explain what it helps us picture.
We try using similar language techniques in our own story writing.
